description: Package Manager (Pacman): The elite package manager utilized natively by Arch Linux, Manjaro, and BlackArch for ultra-fast package synchronization.

commands.explanation: Synchronizes repository databases and updates all out-of-date packages on your Arch system.
Installs a specific package from the synchronized repository.
Searches the repository database for a package matching the specified keyword.
Displays extensive information about a locally installed package.
Removes a package, leaving its dependencies and its configuration files completely intact.
The cleanest removal command. Recursively removes the package, its unused dependencies, and its global configuration files.
Lists all 'orphaned' packages—packages installed as dependencies that are no longer required by any parent package.
Automatically purges all orphaned packages from your system, reclaiming disk space.
Searches for which package containing a specific file (great for fixing missing libraries).
Aggressively clears the pacman cache by removing all cached packages that aren't strictly needed.
